calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Alicia Gamez for Nina Lorenz and Lily Lorenz.

Ms. Gamez stated that her Clients are not pursuing this, that a request for dismissal was filed and that she needs to make sure that the fees that were waived have been payed. The Court noted that the dismissal that was filed dismissed Ms. Lvovich's petition and directed Ms. Gamez to speak with Ms. Lvovich about the waived fees. The Petition for Letters of Administration is off calendar. Ms. Gamez stated that she will file a dismissal.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-20-2019 Continued To Oct-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Milla Lvovich for and with Lenka Lorenz (Petitioner).
Alicia Gamez for Nina Lorenz and Lily Lorenz (Heirs/Children of Decedent).
Magdalena Collett (Decedent's Sister) in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that it has a petition for probate by the Spouse, that the objections nominate Ms. Collett, but there is no petition for appointment of Ms. Collett before it, raised the issue of the Spouse having priority and questions whether there is a basis for not appointing the Spouse. Ms. Gamez stated that the Children would like the Court to provide time for them file their petition and that it is statutorily inappropriate for the Spouse to serve. Ms. Lvovich stated that the Decedent was estranged from his Children, that they have a police report showing that Magdalena has filed with DMV small estate affidavits and has made misrepresentations, asked the Court to set an evidentiary hearing, and raised the issue of Magdalena no longer being represented by an Attorney and not making herself available. The Court offered mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 18, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 22, 2019. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to and the Parties are to return on October 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. If there is going to be a competing petition, it is to be done promptly. The Court expects the Parties to meet and confer, and provide a joint status statement as to where things are by October 22, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
